title: Two STPS officers swear-in their St’at’imc Oath in Tsal’alh
content:
At the St’at’imc Gathering in Tsal’alh, two STPS officers swore their St’at’imc Oath.
Pictured above on the left is Constable Travis Peters and on the right is Cst. Jamie Warren.

title: Canoe Awakening Journey launches for the 4th year
content:
Today marks the 4th anniversary of the STPS Canoe Awakening Journey. Over the winter of 2021-2022, the STPS obtained a 21’ft skipper canoe. In a naming contest, St’at’imc community member Lyle Leo named the canoe, “Tselpu’lm’ecw”, which translates to Daybreak. He then donated the winning prize to the Xaxli’p youth group, who held the 1st
canoe journey mid-summer of 2022. Since that first year, “Tselpu’lm’ecw” has been launched a total of 4 times.

title: STP Board begins task of bringing STPS officer salary into Top-20 of The Repository
content: The STP Board began a months-long discussion in making STPS wages competitive.  Six months later, the officers received a significant raise which placed them in the top-20 of the national salary repository.

title: STPS Constables participate in Holiday Food Drive
content: STPS Officers joined the RCMP in collecting donations for the local food bank by having donors stuff as much as possible into the back of a police cruiser. As well, they donated their own staff luncheon meals catered by Ts’pun Groceries to people in need.

title: STPS Civilian Staff participate in the Rock-Your-Mocs online event
content:
STPS civilian and sworn-staff attended a worldwide Native American & Indigenous Peoples social media unity event,
ROCK YOUR MOCS,
the week of November 10-16, 2024.

title: Memorial Picnic Table installed at the Mt. Currie STPS Detachment
content: A memorial picnic table was installed outside the Mt. Currie STPS detachment building days ahead of the late Sgt. Mike Leo’s headstone ceremony marking the 1st year anniversary of his passing.
